skill.name: release
skill.description: Guide release preparation for a monorepo using version bumps, prefixed git tags, GitHub Actions, and GitHub Releases.
skill.summary: Guide tag-based releases for Stash Downloader and Stash Browser plugins.
skill.author: Codename-11

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many command, network, and environment patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are release workflow instructions in markdown, not executable hidden code, but the skill directs branch changes, merges, version bumps, tag pushes, and GitHub release actions. No prompt injection, obfuscation, credential exfiltration, or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: Release Workflow Executes Repository-Mutating Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs the assistant to run git checkout, git merge, npm version, git tag, and git push commands. This is expected for a release skill, but it can change branches, create commits, push tags, and publish releases if followed without user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and their side effects are clear. The context supports legitimate release automation, so the finding is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Command Substitution Appears In Commit Message Examples
medium_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE for arbitrary command execution: the command substitution builds a multi-line commit message from a quoted heredoc. It does not execute user-controlled input, but it can still be confusing and should be reviewed before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static pattern is real, but semantic context shows a quoted heredoc used as a commit message. No evidence found that untrusted input is interpolated into the command substitution.

medium_findings.title: Environment-Based Release Note Generation Mentioned
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE as a sensitive configuration indicator: the workflow notes that AI release notes are generated if GOOGLE_API_KEY is configured. No exfiltration code is present in this skill file, but users should understand that the downstream GitHub Actions workflow may consume a secret.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key reference is explicit, but the skill only documents downstream workflow behavior. No evidence found that the skill reads, prints, or transmits the secret.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation Links Are Benign
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The detected URLs point to Claude Code attribution text and the repository GitHub Actions page. They do not receive credentials or local data from the skill instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation references. No evidence found of outbound data transfer, webhook use, or credential-bearing requests.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Finding Is A String Match
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography, but the relevant lines contain the skill name and a git tag command, not cryptographic operations. No hashing, encryption, or signature algorithm use was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found release descriptions and git history commands only. No cryptographic API or algorithm selection appears in the file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Git Push And Tag Publication Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes commands that push main, dev, and version tags to origin. These commands are not malicious, but they have publication side effects and require explicit user approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly push branches or tags. The release context is legitimate, but the operational impact is significant.

dangerous_patterns.title: Branch Merge And Version Mutation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow directs branch checkouts, merges, package version updates, and commits. These operations can alter repository state if run in the wrong branch or project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and affect local repository state. They are normal for release work but should be gated by branch and status checks.

content.value_statement: Manual plugin releases can fail when tags, branches, and package versions drift. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured release checklist for the Stash plugin monorepo.
actual_capabilities: Identifies whether Stash Downloader or Stash Browser should be released.
Checks branch, worktree, type-check, lint, test, and build status before release.
Guides version bump selection from commits since the last prefixed tag.
Documents direct tag-based release steps for each plugin.
Explains how to wait for GitHub Actions before syncing the dev branch.
Provides an optional pull request based release path for larger changes.
limitations: Designed for the Codename-11 Stash plugin monorepo, not a generic release system.
Requires working git, npm, GitHub remote, and optional GitHub CLI access.
Does not validate the downstream GitHub Actions workflow implementation.
Can publish branches or tags if commands are executed without review.

use_cases.title: Ship a Plugin Patch
use_cases.description: Follow the checklist, bump the package version, tag the plugin, and trigger the GitHub release workflow.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Reviewed Release
use_cases.description: Create a release branch and pull request before merging, tagging, and publishing the release.

use_cases.title: Recover a Cancelled Deployment
use_cases.description: Re-push a plugin tag after an early dev branch push cancels the stable deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Release
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin a standard release for one plugin.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the release skill to prepare a patch release for Stash Downloader. Confirm the plugin, run the pre-release checks, and show me each command before execution.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Version Bump
prompt_templates.scenario: Review commits and decide the version change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the release workflow to inspect commits since the last browser tag and recommend whether this should be a patch, minor, or major release.

prompt_templates.title: Release With Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use the pull request path for a larger release.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a PR-based release for Stash Browser. Create the release branch, update the version, draft the PR title, and wait for my approval before pushing.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Release State
prompt_templates.scenario: Check whether a release completed correctly.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the release skill to verify the latest Downloader tag, GitHub Actions status, GitHub Release state, and whether dev is synchronized with main.

output_examples.input: Release Stash Downloader as a patch version.
output: Identified Stash Downloader as the target plugin.
Confirmed the release should use the downloader-vX.Y.Z tag format.
Prepared a checklist covering branch, status, tests, build, version bump, tag push, and workflow verification.

output_examples.input: The stable deploy was cancelled after release.
output: Explained that pushing dev too early can cancel the stable workflow.
Outlined recovery by deleting and re-pushing the affected plugin tag.
Included a reminder to wait for GitHub Actions before syncing dev again.

output_examples.input: I want a reviewed release for Browser.
output: Selected the PR-based release path for Stash Browser.
Prepared release branch, version bump, pull request, merge, and tag verification steps.
Separated review steps from publication steps so the maintainer can approve pushes.

best_practices: Confirm the target plugin and exact version before changing files or creating tags.
Run type-check, lint, tests, and build before pushing release branches or tags.
Wait for the stable GitHub Actions workflow to finish before syncing dev from main.
anti_patterns: Do not release both plugins with one shared tag.
Do not push dev immediately after pushing the release tag.
Do not run publication commands before the maintainer approves the version and target branch.

faq.question: Which plugins does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports Stash Downloader and Stash Browser in the Codename-11 Stash plugin monorepo.

faq.question: Does this skill create GitHub Releases directly?
faq.answer: It documents the tag push that triggers GitHub Actions to create the GitHub Release.

faq.question: Why are plugin tags prefixed?
faq.answer: The monorepo contains multiple plugins, so prefixes separate Downloader and Browser release histories.

faq.question: Can I use this for any npm package?
faq.answer: No. The steps reference repository-specific branches, package paths, tag formats, and GitHub Actions behavior.

faq.question: What should I review before running it?
faq.answer: Review the target plugin, version bump, branch state, test results, remote name, and every push command.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it instructs commands that can publish tags, merge branches, and trigger release automation.
